test: add assembly coverage tests for LocationToTrsf and multi-shape loading (#327)

* test: add assembly coverage tests for LocationToTrsf and multi-shape loading

Add three new tests to drive coverage of the previously uncovered
LocationToTrsf function and multi-shape STEP assembly loading:

* FromSTEPTripleBox — loads the triple-box-v1 fixture (three free-shape
  boxes all named 'Component') and verifies that three logical volumes
  with deduplicated names are created.

* AssemblyWithTranslation — builds an XDE assembly programmatically
  using a BRep compound with two instances of the same box, one at the
  origin and one translated by 50 mm on X.  STEPCAFControl_Writer
  exports the assembly structure; on reimport ImportLabel walks the
  component reference labels and calls LocationToTrsf for the translated
  component.  After MakeImprint the two daughter placements are verified
  to differ in X by exactly 50 mm.

* GetLogicalVolumesCompleteness — verifies GetLogicalVolumes() returns
  exactly one entry keyed by the part name for a single-shape STEP.

Also adds includes: BRep_Builder.hxx, TopoDS_Compound.hxx,
TDF_LabelSequence.hxx, TopLoc_Location.hxx, gp_Trsf.hxx, gp_Vec.hxx,
cmath.

* fix(container): disable BUILD_TESTING in Dockerfile; drop yaml-cpp from spack

include(CTest) implicitly sets BUILD_TESTING=ON, which causes cmake to
require yaml-cpp even though no tests are being built in the container.
Add -DBUILD_TESTING=OFF to avoid this.

yaml-cpp is a test-only dependency and is not present in the EIC spack
buildcache (ghcr.io/eic/spack-v2025.11.0), so spack had to build it
from source — lengthening the base-layer build unnecessarily.  Remove
it from spack.yaml so the base image only contains runtime dependencies
(Geant4 and OpenCASCADE).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(container): cache spack base layer in GHCR by spack.yaml hash

base-container.yml:
- Hash .github/spack.yaml (first 8 hex chars of SHA-256) to produce a
  stable tag spack-<hash> that changes only when the environment changes.
- After GHCR login, inspect ghcr.io/eic/g4occt:spack-<hash> to detect a
  cache hit; skip the 44-min spack install + buildcache push on hit.
- On cache miss, push the new image under the hash tag (not :base directly)
  so the immutable content-addressed entry survives future retagging.
- Always retag the hash-tagged image as :base via docker buildx imagetools
  create (manifest-only, no layer download) so downstream steps find it.
- Add docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 (needed for imagetools create).

container.yml:
- Add cache-from/cache-to (type=registry, :buildcache tag) to
  build-push-action so Docker BuildKit layer cache is stored in GHCR and
  reused across builds when only the G4OCCT source changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator (#673)

* feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator

Add support for the com.microsoft::GatherBlockQuantized contrib operator
which combines gather and block dequantization in a single operation.

Supports:
- Packed data (UINT8 containing 4-bit values) and native sub-byte types (INT4, UINT4)
- 4-bit and 8-bit quantization
- Optional zero_points (packed or unpacked)
- Configurable gather_axis and quantize_axis
- Default zero_point of 2^(bits-1) for unsigned data types

commit.message:
ci(clang-tidy): suppress per-file noise and exclude _fetchcontent (#325)

* ci(clang-tidy): suppress per-file noise and exclude _fetchcontent

Two fixes to the 'Run clang-tidy on all files' step (non-PR pushes):

1. Add -quiet to run-clang-tidy so the per-file verbose summary
   ('N warnings generated. Suppressed M warnings (N in non-user code
   ...). Use -header-filter=.* to display errors from all non-system
   headers.') is not printed for every analysed file.

2. Pass '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as the files-regex argument so that
   source files under _fetchcontent/ (googlebenchmark, googletest) are
   excluded from analysis.  compile_commands.json includes those TUs
   because they are built as part of the project, but they are not our
   code and should not be linted.

The 'Run clang-tidy on changed files' step (PR events) already has
-quiet and only analyses files that appear in the PR diff, so it is
unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(clang-tidy): use -source-filter instead of positional regex

The positional 'files' argument to run-clang-tidy is a regex on the
file path (documented as 'Files to be processed (regex on path)' and
implemented with re.search()), so '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as a positional
arg is also correct.  However, the named -source-filter option makes the
intent unambiguous and is less likely to be misread as a literal filename.

-source-filter is applied with re.match() and achieves the same result:
files whose absolute path contains '_fetchcontent' are excluded from
analysis; all other project source files are analysed as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions (#323)

* fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions

CMake captures the directory COMPILE_OPTIONS into each target's property
at the point the target is created (add_library / add_executable).  The
sanitizer and coverage blocks were placed after all library and test
target definitions, so add_compile_options() / add_link_options() had no
effect on any of those targets.

Move all USE_ASAN, USE_UBSAN, USE_TSAN, and USE_COVERAGE blocks to
immediately after the compiler-warning options and before any target or
find_package() calls.  This ensures --coverage, -fsanitize=*, etc. are
applied to G4OCCT, G4OCCTFixtureSupport, all test executables, and the
standalone app.

Verified locally: after the fix the coverage report covers 1118/1366
lines (81.8%) and 112/116 functions (96.6%), up from the broken 4/7
(57.1%) that only reflected inline header methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix(tests): delete G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler in TearDown; suppress Geant4 vis leaks

The fix to CMakeLists.txt that moves sanitizer options before target
definitions now correctly instruments all test executables with ASAN/UBSAN.
Two classes of new LeakSanitizer failures are addressed here:

1. G4OCCTTestEnv::TearDown() leak (unit tests 1–92)
   SetUp() installs a heap-allocated G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler via
   G4StateManager::SetExceptionHandler(), which does not take ownership.
   TearDown() restored the previous handler but never deleted ours, leaking
   16 bytes per test run.  Fix: retrieve the current handler before restoring
   the previous one, then delete it.

2. Geant4 RayTracer / PhysicalVolumeModel / scene leaks (vis tests 138–180)
   Geant4's visualization infrastructure (G4RayTracerViewer, G4OutBitStream,
   G4PhysicalVolumeModel::CreateCurrentAttValues, G4Scene::AddWorldIfEmpty,
   G4VisCommandSceneAddVolume::SetNewValue, etc.) allocates objects that are
   intentionally not freed at process exit.  These are upstream Geant4 leaks,
   not G4OCCT bugs.  Add suppressions to .github/lsan.supp covering all
   stack frames seen in the CI run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve tab completions grid rendering logic (#365)

Implements four improvements to the tab completion grid rendering:

## Changes Made

- **Early "No suggestions" exit**: When there are zero filtered
suggestions, the "No suggestions" message is printed immediately at the
top of the render block and the grid-building logic is skipped entirely.

- **Middle-ellipsis truncation for wide suggestions**: If a suggestion's
text is wider than the terminal width and it falls in the first visible
column, `render()` now applies middle truncation (e.g.
`very_long_path_name` → `very_l…ame`) instead of silently discarding the
column.

- **2D selected position**: `ActiveSuggestions` now tracks
`(selected_row, selected_col)` instead of a flat index. Up/down arrows
navigate within a column; left/right arrows jump between columns. If
navigating right would land on an empty cell in a partial last column,
the selection wraps back to column 0.

- **Column scroll offset**: A `col_scroll_offset` field ensures the
selected column is always visible. `into_grid()` accepts a `col_offset`
parameter to skip leading columns, and the offset is updated after each
render and eagerly on navigation so there is no visible lag.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Tab completions grid improvements:
> if the number of active suggestions is zero, the print the No
suggestions at the start of the render block and exit that block.
> 
> If the suggestion is too wide to fit onto a single line and we are
drawing the first column, the remove the middle part of the suggestion
and replace it with the ellipses character.
> 
> Keep track of the selected position as a 2d value, not just an index.
When you move to the right, increase the column index. The in the index
check function, if there isn't a suggestion for that 2d pos, set col to
0 etc.
> 
> If the cursor goes too far to the right, we currently wont be able to
draw this column. So we should skip some columns so that the column with
the cursor is always drawn.


</details>

* Add KL rollback to prevent catastrophic policy updates

When approx_kl exceeds target_kl * 4 (default: 0.08), the entire
optimization round is discarded and model weights are restored to
their pre-optimization state. This prevents the cascading failure
observed in weighted circuit training where a single bad batch
caused a 10x KL spike, destroying the value function and collapsing
progress from 14 to 0.

The rollback threshold is configurable via kl_rollback multiplier
(default 4.0x target_kl). A losses/kl_rollback metric is logged
to tensorboard for visibility.
